steps.title: Use the nevertheless contrast
steps.content: The text contrasts two facts: researchers have not deciphered the civilization's writing system, but artifacts still reveal details about customs and community organization. The conclusion after therefore must follow from that contrast.

steps.title: Reject unsupported overclaims
steps.content: Choice A adds an unsupported claim about investigation being easier. Choice C prescribes research priorities the text never discusses. Choice D contradicts the claim that the language remains unidentified. The supported conclusion is that language knowledge is not necessary for learning civilization details. The answer is B.
